title: 10 Herbal Teas to Reclaim Your Voice as Coaches with Lost Voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been used for centuries to help soothe a lost voice. The main reason for this is due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and vocal cords. The tea contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins, which work together to calm irritation and promote healing. If you're experiencing a lost voice, try making a tea by steeping dried licorice root in hot water for 5-7 minutes. You can drink it up to 3 times a day. To get the most out of the tea, it's recommended to drink it warm or at room temperature, rather than ice-cold. Some people also find it helpful to gargle with the tea before swallowing to help soothe the throat directly. When taking licorice root tea, you can expect to see improvements in your vocal cord inflammation and soreness. You may start to feel a reduction in discomfort within a few hours, and your voice may begin to return to normal within a day or two. However, it's essential to note that if your lost voice persists or is accompanied by other concerning symptoms, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas are a popular remedy for lost voice, and for good reason. The root of the Echinacea plant is rich in compounds like alkylamides, glycoproteins, and polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help soothe and protect the vocal cords, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The plant also contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have a positive effect on the immune system, helping to fight off infections that may be causing the lost voice. To use Echinacea purpurea teas for a lost voice, start by brewing 1-2 teaspoons of dried root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups a day, and consider adding honey or lemon to taste. As the tea works to calm and heal the vocal cords, you may notice a reduction in throat discomfort and an improvement in your ability to speak. You may also experience a faster recovery time, with your voice returning to normal within a few days. However, if your lost voice persists or is accompanied by other symptoms like a fever or difficulty swallowing, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, has been used for centuries to help soothe a lost voice. The reason behind its effectiveness lies in its rich properties, particularly its high content of thymol, a natural antiseptic and antibacterial compound. Thymol helps to reduce inflammation in the throat, which can cause hoarseness and loss of voice. Additionally, thyme tea contains mucilages, which are soothing agents that calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. To use thymus vulgaris teas for a lost voice, you can make a tea by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and drink it while it's still warm. You can also add a bit of honey to make it more palatable. It's recommended to drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm the throat. The likely outcomes from taking thymus vulgaris teas for a lost voice include a reduction in throat irritation and discomfort, allowing you to speak more comfortably and with a clearer voice. You may also notice an improvement in your vocal range and tone. However, it's essential to note that thyme tea may not restore your original voice immediately, but it can help alleviate the symptoms and support your vocal recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been used for centuries to soothe a lost voice. This is due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce swelling in the throat and fight off infection. Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. When you drink ginger tea, these compounds work together to ease throat congestion, reduce inflammation, and promote healing.  To use ginger tea for a lost voice, try making a tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water for 5-7 minutes. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be careful not to strain your vocal cords further by shouting or talking loudly. Instead, rest your voice as much as possible and drink the tea 2-3 times a day. As the tea works its way through your system, you may start to feel a reduction in throat discomfort and a gradual improvement in your voice. Within a few days, your voice should return to normal, and you may be able to speak and sing without any strain.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been used to help soothe a lost voice due to its anti-inflammatory and calming properties. The tea contains constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their ability to reduce swelling and promote relaxation. When you drink Lavandula angustifolia tea, the active compounds help to calm your vocal cords and reduce inflammation, making it easier to speak and sing.  To use Lavandula angustifolia tea for a lost voice, make a strong brew by steeping one teaspoon of dried flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool down before drinking it. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of tea a day to get the desired effects.  Taking action with Lavandula angustifolia tea may lead to a decrease in vocal cord inflammation, reduced swelling, and improved vocal quality. You might start to feel a relief from your sore throat and a slight improvement in your ability to speak or sing within a few hours of drinking the tea. As you continue to drink the tea, you may notice a significant improvement in your vocal range and tone, allowing you to sing or speak with more confidence.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense teas, also known as red clover tea, can help with lost voice due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea contains isoflavones, such as formononetin and genistein, which have been shown to reduce swelling in the throat and promote healing. The mucilages in red clover tea also help to calm irritated tissues and soothe the throat.  To use red clover tea for a lost voice, you can try the following: - Steep a teaspoon of dried red clover flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. - Strain the tea and drink it warm or at room temperature. - You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to choose a honey that's suitable for your diet. - Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to see the best results. When you drink red clover tea for a lost voice, you may experience a reduction in throat inflammation, a decrease in pain and discomfort, and an improvement in your vocal quality. As your throat heals, you should start to feel more comfortable speaking and singing. The tea may also help to reduce the risk of further irritation and promote a faster recovery. However, if your lost voice persists or is accompanied by other symptoms, such as difficulty swallowing or shortness of breath, you should seek medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Trifolium pratense herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been used to help with lost voice due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea contains constituents like astragalosides, saponins, and flavonoids that help reduce swelling and soothe the vocal cords. Astragalosides, in particular, have been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membranes, which can become inflamed and irritated when we lose our voice.  To use Astragalus membranaceus teas to help with lost voice, try drinking a cup of the tea 2-3 times a day. Steep one teaspoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. You can also add honey or lemon to make the tea more palatable. Some people find it helpful to gargle with the tea after drinking it to further soothe the throat. When you take Astragalus membranaceus teas, you may notice a reduction in inflammation and irritation in the throat, which can help your voice to return to normal. You may also find that your throat feels more comfortable and less sore. In some cases, the tea may help to stimulate the production of mucus, which can help to clear out irritants and debris from the throat. However, if your lost voice is caused by a underlying medical condition, such as acid reflux or a viral infection, you may need to see a doctor for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been used for centuries to help with lost voice. The tea's active properties, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation and soothe the throat. These constituents also have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may be causing the loss of voice. To use Melissa officinalis teas for lost voice, start by brewing one teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves in a cup of hot water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink it several times a day. You can also add honey to soothe the throat further. As you take the tea, you may start to feel your throat relaxing, and your vocal cords may begin to function normally again. Within a few days, your voice should start to return, and you may be able to speak more clearly and with more volume. However, if your lost voice persists or is accompanied by other symptoms such as difficulty swallowing or shortness of breath, you should consult a doctor to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, may help with a lost voice due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea contains menthol, menthone, and limonene, which can help to calm the inflammation in the vocal cords and throat. When you have a lost voice, it's usually because of an irritation or inflammation in these areas. Drinking Mentha x piperita tea may help to reduce this inflammation, allowing your vocal cords to heal and your voice to return. To try this remedy, start by brewing a strong cup of Mentha x piperita tea and letting it cool down. Then, gargle with the tea several times to help soothe the throat and vocal cords. You can also drink the tea as you normally would, but it's best to do this when you're not trying to talk or sing. For best results, try this remedy as soon as you feel your voice starting to go, rather than waiting until it's completely gone. It's also a good idea to stay hydrated by drinking plenty of water and avoiding irritants like smoke and dust. When you drink Mentha x piperita tea for a lost voice, you may start to feel a reduction in inflammation and discomfort in the throat and vocal cords within a few hours. Your voice may also start to return, although it may take a few days for it to fully recover. Keep in mind that this remedy may not work for everyone, and if your lost voice persists or is accompanied by other symptoms like difficulty swallowing or shortness of breath, you should see a doctor to rule out any underlying conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for lost voice

remedies.remedy_desc: Hedera helix teas, also known as English ivy tea, may help with lost voice due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea contains constituents like saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to reduce swelling and promote healing in the throat and vocal cords. When you're struggling with a lost voice, try making a warm tea by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried Hedera helix leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day, ideally between meals. You can also add honey to soothe your throat. As you drink the tea, you may experience a reduction in throat inflammation, a decrease in vocal strain, and improved vocal clarity. Your voice may begin to return to normal within a few days, although this can vary depending on the underlying cause of your lost voice.
remedies.image_alt: Hedera helix herbal teas for lost voice

intro_desc: Coaches often have to scream and shout at the top of their lungs to motivate their players and get their message across. But this repeated strain on their vocal cords can lead to a lost voice, making it hard for them to speak, let alone shout. This can be a real problem for coaches, as they need to communicate effectively with their team and other people involved in the game. A lost voice can also affect their confidence and ability to do their job, causing stress and anxiety. Imagine having to cancel a game or practice because you can't speak - it's a nightmare for any coach.  A lost voice can also have a big impact on a coach's daily life. They may struggle to explain things to their family and friends, or even to order food at a restaurant. Simple tasks become difficult, and it can be frustrating and isolating. Coaches may also worry about the long-term effects of a lost voice, such as permanent damage to their vocal cords.  When coaches lose their voice, they often try all sorts of things to get it back, from rest and relaxation to medication and even surgery. But one natural and effective way to help restore a lost voice is through herbal teas. These teas are made from plants that have been used for centuries to soothe and heal the body. They can help to calm the vocal cords, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. By drinking herbal teas, coaches can give their vocal cords a break and help them recover from the strain of shouting and screaming.
